scored_hypotheses.claim: The Voynich Manuscript encodes a late medieval Moravian (Old Czech/Slavic) dialect using a construct

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: While Zipfian properties (exponent 0.8946) indicate natural language-like structure, the hapax ratio of 0.701 is unusually high for authentic natural language texts and inconsistent with Moravian. The statistics do not specifically validate the Moravian cipher claim, Gujarati plant-name assertion, or medicinal decoding on f14v without evidence of consistent decoding across other folios as required.

scored_hypotheses.claim: The Voynich Manuscript is a trilingual pharmaceutical compendium encoding plant-based medicine in a

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: The high hapax ratio (0.701) contradicts the 168-term dictionary hypothesis; 80% coverage would require a much lower hapax ratio (~0.3-0.4) and fewer unique words (8,261 is too high). The Zipfian exponent supports natural language but not an artificially constrained vocabulary system.

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
